query: My XGBoost gets 0.92 ROC-AUC on the test set. Is that good?
expected_behavior: Refuses to call 0.92 'good' (or otherwise certify it) without baseline-comparison context
Requests the Dummy and / or linear-baseline (LogisticRegression) ROC-AUC on the same test set before rendering a verdict
Explains that lift over baseline is the right framing for whether a complex model is earning its complexity — not the absolute number alone
